title: Nicolas Cage on Why Christopher Nolan and Other Directors Won’t Work With Him After He Turned Down Their Films: ‘They Get Their Feelings Hurt’
content:
ByPat Saperstein

Deputy Editor

Nicolas Cageis still remembering that he might have blown a big chance when he turned down a role inChristopher Nolan‘s “Insomnia.”

The Oscar-winning actor covered numerous pivotal moments in his career in an antic, wide-ranging interview with theNew York Timesahead of the premiere of his “Spider-Noir” series on MGM+, and recalled that several renowned auteurs seem to have lost his number after he declined roles in their films.

“David O. Russell offered me a movie a million years ago. It was a good movie, and he offered it and I said no, and he’s the only director that I ever said no to who actually came back and offered me another movie,” Cage told the Times. “Most of them, they get their feelings hurt and don’t call you back. It’s happened a million times to me. It’s happened with Christopher Nolan, it’s happened with Woody Allen, it’s happened with Paul Thomas Anderson. They don’t call me back.”

Cage went on to say that the Nolan movie was the 2002 “Insomnia.” Russell, however, did offer him another movie — “Madden,” about NFL coach John Madden, which is slated for a November release.

“Anyway, David did call me, and it showed a lot of class that he would call me back and invite me again, and I didn’t want to say no to him again because I have great respect for his talent,” Cage said. “And it was a beautiful experience. I enjoyed working with David. I enjoyed working with Christian [Bale], John Mulaney. But it was a big challenge. I don’t think of myself when I think of John Madden. So I was like, OK, how can I get way out of my comfort zone? Which is what David Bowie said to me. I asked him, ‘How did you keep reinventing yourself?’ He said, ‘I just never got comfortable with anything I was doing.'”

“Spider-Noir,” based on the Marvel comic, premieres in the U.S. on MGM+ Monday and on Prime Video globally on Wednesday. Cage plays 1930s private investigator Ben Reilly who is also a web-slinging superhero. The series is available to watch in either black and white or color.
title_zh: 尼古拉斯·凱奇(Nicolas Cage)談到為何克里斯多福·諾蘭(Christopher Nolan)及其他導演在他拒絕了他們的電影後不會與他合作：「他們會感到受傷。」
content_zh:
由帕特·薩佩斯坦(Pat Saperstein)

副編輯

尼古拉斯·凱奇(Nicolas Cage)仍然記得他可能錯過了一個大機會，因為他拒絕了克里斯多福·諾蘭(Christopher Nolan)的《失眠症》(Insomnia)中的一個角色。

這位奧斯卡獲獎演員在接受《紐約時報》(New York Times)的廣泛訪談時，回顧了他職業生涯中的許多關鍵時刻，並提到幾位著名導演似乎在他拒絕了他們的電影角色後失去了他的聯繫方式。

“大衛·O·拉塞爾(David O. Russell)在一百萬年前給我提供了一部電影。那是一部好電影，他提出了邀約，我卻拒絕了，他是我唯一拒絕過的導演，之後還真的回來再給我提供了一部電影，”凱奇告訴《紐約時報》。 “大多數導演都會受傷，不再聯繫你。這種情況發生過無數次。克里斯多福·諾蘭、伍迪·艾倫(Woody Allen)、保羅·托馬斯·安德森(Paul Thomas Anderson)都曾經這樣。他們不再聯繫我。”

凱奇接著說，諾蘭的電影是2002年的《失眠症》(Insomnia)。然而，拉塞爾確實給他提供了另一部電影——《麥登》(Madden)，這部電影講述了NFL教練約翰·麥登(John Madden)，預計將於11月上映。

“無論如何，大衛確實聯繫了我，這顯示了他的風度，他會再次聯繫我並再次邀請我，我不想再拒絕他，因為我非常尊重他的才華，”凱奇說。“這是一個美好的經歷。我喜歡和大衛合作。我喜歡和克里斯蒂安·貝爾(Christian Bale)、約翰·穆蘭尼(John Mulaney)合作。但這是一個巨大的挑戰。我在想約翰·麥登時並不會想到自己。所以我想，好的，我該怎麼樣才能走出我的舒適區？這正是大衛·鮑伊(David Bowie)告訴我的。我問他，‘你是怎麼不斷重塑自己的？’他說，‘我從來沒有對我正在做的事情感到舒適。’”

基於漫威漫畫的《蜘蛛黑色》(Spider-Noir)將於週一在美國的MGM+首播，並於週三在全球的Prime Video上線。凱奇飾演1930年代的私人偵探本·瑞利(Ben Reilly)，他同時也是一位網絡超級英雄。該系列可供觀眾選擇以黑白或彩色觀看。
summary_zh:
由帕特·薩佩斯坦(Pat Saperstein)

副編輯

尼古拉斯·凱奇(Nicolas Cage)仍然記得他可能錯過了一個大機會，因為他拒絕了克里斯多福·諾蘭(Christopher Nolan)的《失眠症》(Insomnia)中的一個角色。
